cards.back: A two-dimensional table of data with columns of potentially different types

cards.front: What does the .head() method in Pandas do?
cards.back: Returns the first few rows of a DataFrame

cards.front: What is the difference between drop() and dropna() methods in Pandas?
cards.back: drop() removes specified rows or columns by label or index, while dropna() removes rows or columns with missing values

cards.front: What is the purpose of the groupby() function in Pandas?
cards.back: It groups the data by one or more columns and performs aggregation operations on the grouped data

cards.front: What does the .merge() method in Pandas do?
cards.back: It combines data from two or more DataFrames based on a common column
